// ==== koine/generated/billing/Currency.java ====
// <auto-generated/>
package koine.generated.billing;

/**
 * Currencies with their ISO code and minor-unit count.
 */
public enum Currency {
    EUR("EUR", 2L),
    USD("USD", 2L);

    private final String code;
    private final long decimals;

    private Currency(String code, long decimals) {
        this.code = code;
        this.decimals = decimals;
    }

    public String code() {
        return this.code;
    }

    public long decimals() {
        return this.decimals;
    }
}

// ==== koine/generated/billing/Email.java ====
// <auto-generated/>
package koine.generated.billing;

/**
 * An email address, shape-validated.
 */
public record Email(String raw) {
    public Email {
        if (!(java.util.regex.Pattern.compile("^[^@]+@[^@]+$").matcher(raw).find())) {
            throw new koine.runtime.DomainException("invalid email address");
        }
    }
}

// ==== koine/generated/billing/Money.java ====
// <auto-generated/>
package koine.generated.billing;

/**
 * A monetary amount in a currency. Never negative.
 */
public record Money(java.math.BigDecimal amount, Currency currency) {
    public Money {
        if (!(amount.compareTo(java.math.BigDecimal.ZERO) >= 0)) {
            throw new koine.runtime.DomainException("a monetary amount cannot be negative");
        }
    }
}

// ==== koine/runtime/DomainException.java ====
// <auto-generated/>
package koine.runtime;

/**
 * Thrown when a domain invariant is violated. Unchecked ({@code RuntimeException}) so generated
 * value objects, entities, and aggregates can guard their invariants without checked-exception
 * ceremony — the Java analogue of the C# emitter's DomainInvariantViolationException.
 *
 * <p>Generated by Koine — do not edit by hand.</p>
 */
public class DomainException extends RuntimeException {
    public DomainException(String message) {
        super(message);
    }
}
